message:
chore(beads): close franken_numpy-qhmv (fixed in 45d81cb)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"franken_numpy-qd84","title":"numpy.positive python wrapper parity","description":"Add fnp_python.positive passthrough wrapper around numpy.positive(x). Returns +x element-wise (a copy). Include oracle test covering: float scalar, negative scalar, 1-D mixed-sign array, integer input, complex input, 2-D array, and dtype preservation across float/int/complex.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:33:26.442663085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:47:21.808348346Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:47:21.806427907Z","close_reason":"Closed by cc-numpy — wrapper + oracle parity test delivered in commit 764f448 (verified via git log --grep br-franken_numpy-qd84).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"franken_numpy-qhmv","title":"[HIGH] fnp-python ma_count test: AttributeError on direct .dtype on scalar int result","description":"fnp-python test tests::ma_count_matches_numpy_across_axis_and_keepdims FAILS with AttributeError: 'int' object has no attribute 'dtype'.

## Reproducer

export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_numpy_cc
cargo test -p fnp-python --lib ma_count_matches_numpy_across_axis_and_keepdims

## Root cause

np.ma.count with axis=None returns a plain Python int (not a 0-D numpy array). The test body at lib.rs (multiple call sites) uses direct .getattr("dtype")?.str()?.to_string() pattern instead of the scalar-tolerant assert_array_matches_numpy helper that ma_count's OTHER test paths use.

Context: commit a61593e (cc-numpy, 2026-04-22) made the central assert_array_matches_numpy helper scalar-tolerant by checking .getattr("dtype").is_err() before recursing. That fix resolves the helper-based assertions, but does NOT help inline .getattr("dtype")? calls that the test has for extra dtype-name assertions.

## Suggested fix

Either:
(a) Guard each inline .dtype call with a scalar check:
    if ours_int.getattr("dtype").is_ok() {
        let ours_dtype = ours_int.getattr("dtype")?.str()?.to_string();
        ...
    }

(b) Extract inline dtype assertions into a helper that mirrors assert_array_matches_numpy's scalar tolerance.

(c) Route ma_count's axis=None case through a different assertion path that doesn't check dtype (since Python ints don't have one).

## Severity rationale

[HIGH] — this is a pure test-side bug (the wrapper itself returns the correct Python int matching numpy). Blocks the test battery from being green. Masks possible regressions in wrapper behavior because the test fails early on the axis=None case.

## Ownership

Test body added by cod-numpy during hswc rust-port work. Fix is mechanical — apply the scalar-tolerance pattern to inline dtype asserts (4-5 sites in this test).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:43:01.433456511Z","created_by":"cc-numpy","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:43:01.433456511Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review","scalar-tolerant","test-bug"]}

Fix is mechanical — apply the scalar-tolerance pattern to inline dtype asserts (4-5 sites in this test).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:43:01.433456511Z","created_by":"cc-numpy","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:50:15.486414105Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:50:15.484543957Z","close_reason":"Closed by cc-numpy — fixed in commit 45d81cb. Root cause: assert_array_matches_numpy helper only handled symmetric scalar-both-sides case; asymmetric ours=numpy-scalar theirs=python-int threw AttributeError on shape lookup. Fix: added .item() normalisation so both sides compare via repr after reducing to Python scalar form. Verified: ma_count_matches_numpy_across_axis_and_keepdims 1 ok / 0 failed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review","scalar-tolerant","test-bug"]}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-qjjh","title":"Add multi-axis expand_dims support (NumPy 1.18+)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-31T20:58:34.513976983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T21:00:52.702597749Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T21:00:52.702155041Z","close_reason":"Added expand_dims_axes for multi-axis insertion (NumPy 1.18+).  {"id":"franken_numpy-qkbf","title":"Restore fail-fast invariant handling in unique_counts/unique_inverse/unique_all","description":"Current worktree change in crates/fnp-ufunc/src/lib.rs replaces impossible-state unwrap_or_else(unreachable!) guards in unique_counts/unique_inverse/unique_all with silent empty-array fallbacks. These wrappers request the corresponding arrays from unique_with_info, so returning empty arrays would mask internal invariant breaks and violate NumPy-style semantics.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-29T01:56:24.561784002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-29T01:58:08.648898780Z","closed_at":"2026-03-29T01:58:08.648641733Z","close_reason":"Restored fail-fast invariant handling in unique_counts/unique_inverse/unique_all; cargo fmt --check and rch exec cargo test -p fnp-ufunc unique_with_info -- --nocapture passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
